payload.title: Lift MCP doctrine grounding command package
payload.summary: Operator observed that the available Narada proper MCP grounding tools do not include the right doctrine-grounding command for design questions such as Site Registry ownership and hosted projection authority. Request a lift package from narada.architect to specify and implement a doctrine-grounding MCP command/package.
payload.problem: Current MCP tools can report site/fabric context, but they do not retrieve or synthesize relevant doctrine grounding from SEMANTICS.md and docs/concepts/product docs for a question. This led to weak grounding when discussing whether a Cloudflare-hosted Site Registry should be owned by narada-proper, narada-andrey/User Site, or another locus.
requested_work: Specify the doctrine-grounding MCP affordance: input question/topic, optional target Site/locus, doctrine corpus selection, and bounded output contract.
Ground the command in existing Narada doctrine docs rather than creating a second authority source.
Implement through Narada proper MCP facade as read-only/facade-only unless explicitly admitted otherwise.
Include tests proving it returns doctrine refs/summaries without mutating Site/task/inbox state and without overclaiming authority.
Document when to use the command for architecture/design grounding and how it differs from site_context/fabric_context.
acceptance_criteria: MCP command exists for doctrine grounding or a clear lift package specifies why not yet implementable.
Output includes doctrine references and bounded synthesis, not free-floating model opinion.
Tests prove read-only posture and relevant doctrine selection.
The original Site Registry ownership question can be re-run through the command as a proof case.
